title: The Ten Greatest (my favorite) Horror Films of All Time (IMO)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=87Vb6sa1KcY

Daniel (@dungeondive) delivers the episode he's been waiting all year to record: his top ten horror films of all time. He's upfront about his tastes — a self-described populist who prizes infinite rewatchability over "elevated horror" — and he structures the countdown as a game, having given Dungeon Dive patrons a single keyword per film ("gross out", "mood", "cosmic", "drama") and reading out their guesses before each reveal. The list runs: The Fly, Carnival of Souls, Sinister, Freddy vs. Jason, The Empty Man, The Exorcist III, Doctor Sleep, An American Werewolf in London, The Thing, and — at number one, a perfect film he rewatches every year — Poltergeist. The fun is in the defences. He mounts a genuinely persuasive case for Freddy vs. Jason as a brilliantly written love letter to both franchises, champions The Empty Man as a buried cosmic-horror masterpiece with one of the worst trailers ever cut, and explains the strange symmetry of loving The Shining novel but not Kubrick's film, while disliking the Doctor Sleep novel and adoring Mike Flanagan's adaptation. Sinister, he confesses, is the only movie ever to scare him as an adult.

----

Daniel predicts flack for Freddy vs. Jason and Doctor Sleep. Which pick on his list would you fight him over — and what's the unapologetically populist favourite on your own top ten?
